description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a camouflage paint scheme featuring green and tan patterns, accented by red star insignias on the tail and fuselage. The aircraft has two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically, connected by struts and wires, typical of early 20th-century military planes. The nose houses a visible radial piston engine with a two-blade propeller, and the landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tailwheel. The tail section features a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a conventional tail design. The cockpit is positioned forward on the fuselage and appears to be open, suitable for a single pilot. The overall construction suggests a lightweight frame, likely made of metal and fabric, designed for agility and short takeoff and landing capabilities.
description: A vintage biplane with green and tan camouflage, red star insignias, dual stacked wings, a visible radial engine, and an open forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the dual stacked wings, camouflage pattern, and red star insignias on the tail and fuselage.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the radial engine, two-blade propeller, and the symmetrical biplane wing structure.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view from the opposite side, again showing the wing configuration and camouflage, with the tailwheel visible.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, single vertical stabilizer, and the alignment of the wings and fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted radial piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a small tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit suitable for a single pilot.
